title: Platform Spine — extract shared template primitives after Phase 2
problem: Two templates (SND + Volunteer Roster) shipped on top of feat-fleet-foundation in Phase 1 + 2. Now we can observe what actually duplicated between them and extract the shared spine deliberately, instead of guessing. The duplications worth extracting: (1) the auth/tenant gate boilerplate at the top of every API handler — both templates write the same six-line pattern of requireTenant + templateId check + currentEmail + ownerEmail/isAdmin compute; (2) the buyer-app chrome (head bar with display name + settings link + email, main wrapper) — copy-pasted between SndApp and VolunteerApp under the .snd-app class names; (3) the empty-state and form-panel layout pattern. After this pass, adding template #3 should be smaller than #2 was, and the parts of feat-fleet-foundation that "felt right" get formal API surfaces (e.g. requireTemplate, AppShell) instead of being implicit copy-paste rules.
analysis: After SND (feat-2026-021) and Volunteer Roster (feat-2026-022) both shipped, the duplication is concrete enough to extract cleanly. Server-side: every template handler opens with the same six-line gate — requireTenant, templateId comparison, currentEmail null-guard, then a derived isOwner from ownerEmail or (isAdmin && isOperator). I extract this into requireTemplate(ctx, templateId, { mustBeOwner? }) which returns { tenant, email, isOwner } or a Response. Client-side: the buyer-app chrome (head bar with displayName + Settings nav + email, max-width main wrapper) is copy-pasted between SndApp and VolunteerApp under the .snd-app class names. I extract this into <AppShell title? children />. Both templates pass the same head shape, so the shell hosts it. The empty-state pattern (dashed-border container with grey text) is small enough to leave inline; not worth a component. The form-panel pattern reuses the existing .admin-block class — already shared, no change needed. After this commit, a third template is provably easier: gate handler in one line, render shell in one wrapper, focus on domain logic.

decisions.decision: Server gate is one helper, two modes — readOnly (any signed-in user) vs ownerOnly
decisions.rationale: The two callers we have today split exactly this way: list/read endpoints and signup-style write endpoints take any signed-in user; create/destroy endpoints take owner only. Encoding the mode as a boolean flag rather than two separate functions keeps the call site obvious and avoids inventing four near-duplicate helpers when needs grow.
alternativesConsidered: Three helpers (anyAuthed, owner, operator) — more API, same behavior
Just inline the boilerplate (rejected: this is the duplication we're here to remove)

decisions.decision: Client AppShell is a tiny chrome component, not a render-prop list framework
decisions.rationale: We have two templates with structurally similar lists but different domain logic. A 'TenantListShell' that took render-props would force both templates through the same data-fetching pattern — SND fetches one endpoint, Volunteer fetches two and joins. AppShell handles only the unambiguously-shared chrome and gets out of the way for the body, keeping each template's domain logic self-contained.

decisions.decision: Class names stay .snd-app-* for now (don't rename to .app-shell-*)
decisions.rationale: The existing CSS works for both templates because Volunteer Roster reuses .snd-app-clip etc. Renaming would touch ~10 files for zero behavioral win and risk breakage during the Phase 3 ship. The renames belong in a follow-up cosmetic pass once a third template forces the issue.
alternativesConsidered: Rename CSS to .app-shell-* in this commit (premature; touches a lot of HTML)

implementation: functions/_lib/template.ts exports requireTemplate(ctx, templateId, opts?) which composes requireTenant + templateId 404 + currentEmail unauthorized + ownerOnly forbidden in one call. Replaces gateBuyerOrOperator (snd/clips.ts) and gateAuthed (volunteer/shifts.ts) and inline checks in volunteer/signups.ts. src/components/AppShell.tsx exports <AppShell> rendering the buyer-app head + main wrapper + footer. SndApp and VolunteerApp shrink by ~30 lines each as they delegate the chrome. No behavior change — every test case from feat-2026-021 and feat-2026-022 still passes.
successCriteria: After this commit, each template's API handler entrypoint is 1 line of gate code (vs ~6 before), and each template's React page renders only its domain content (chrome lives in AppShell). Both /demo/sunday-night-dread (static showroom) and the productized SND + Volunteer Roster surfaces continue to render and behave identically to before. Adding a hypothetical third template would touch only template-specific files — no edits to spine code.
userGuide: Internal refactor; no user-visible changes. For Marc when adding a third template: write your handlers calling requireTemplate(ctx, 'your-template', { ownerOnly: true }) at the top, wrap your page component in <AppShell>{...}</AppShell>, focus on the domain logic in between.
knownLimitations: AppShell title is a string only — logo URL rendering and richer chrome (notifications, role indicators) is deferred until a template actually needs it.
The .snd-app-* CSS class names persist for back-compat; rename to .app-shell-* is a future cosmetic pass.
requireTemplate doesn't memoize the templateId match — trivial cost (string compare), but a generation-3 platform might cache.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Initial fill ahead of build — captures Phase 3 generalization scope and the deliberate-not-renaming decision.
